step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("thyroglossal duct cyst histology")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed review of the provided literature sources, there is clear consensus that thyroglossal duct cysts do indeed contain stratified squamous or respiratory epithelium, contradicting Option B stating otherwise. Furthermore, several references confirm that thyroglossal duct cysts predominantly locate below the hyoid bone ("infrahyoid"), challenging Option C suggesting only 40% reside there. Lastly, while Options A and D correctly identify infections and congenital origins respectively, no reliable source disputes them as false claims. Therefore, I conclude my agreement with prior assessments pointing to Option B as erroneous information regarding the cellular composition of thyroglossal duct cyst walls.
